seo.title: Building Workflow Automation with Llama Stack + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build workflow automation with Llama Stack + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Llama Stack provides a standardized Python interface for building sophisticated Llama-powered agents with built-in safety, memory, and tool capabilities, making it ideal for workflow automation use cases. Workflow automation agents handle multi-step tasks with API integrations, conditional logic, and human approvals, where failures can cascade across business processes. Vex guardrails ensure action correctness, permission validation, and rollback safety, preventing costly errors in production workflows.

architectureOverview.heading: Workflow Automation Architecture with Llama Stack
architectureOverview.description:
The typical Llama Stack workflow automation architecture features an AgentRuntime that orchestrates multi-turn conversations with tools, memory retrieval, and safety filtering. External API integrations are handled via structured tool calls, while conditional logic routes tasks through approval gates or parallel execution paths. Human-in-the-loop steps pause execution for manual review, with state persisted in the memory bank for resumption.

Key components include the Safety Shield for content filtering, ToolExecutor for API calls, and MemoryBank for conversation history and document retrieval. The agent loop processes user requests through planning, tool execution, and response generation, with Vex verification wrapped around each turn to validate outputs before committing actions.

This setup enables reliable automation of complex workflows like order processing, customer onboarding, or IT ticket resolution, where idempotency and rollback are critical.

risks.name: Safety shield false positives
risks.description: Benign workflow instructions blocked by overzealous content filtering
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies intent before safety shield processing with allowlist patterns

risks.name: Agent loop exhaustion
risks.description: Turn limits exceeded without task completion due to poor planning
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors progress metrics and triggers early termination/rollback

risks.name: Malformed tool responses
risks.description: API responses not matching expected Pydantic schemas break workflow
risks.mitigation: Vex schema validation + fallback tool calls with retry logic

risks.name: Memory retrieval hallucination
risks.description: Incorrect document associations lead to wrong conditional routing
risks.mitigation: Vex validates memory context relevance before routing decisions

implementation.title: Initialize Llama Stack AgentRuntime
implementation.description: Set up the core agent with safety, memory, and tools for workflow execution
implementation.code:
from llama_stack.client import AgentRuntime
from llama_stack.model import Model
from llama_stack.safety import SafetyConfig

from vex import verify

# Initialize runtime
runtime = AgentRuntime(
    model=Model(model_id="llama-3.2-11b-avision-preview"),
    safety_config=SafetyConfig(
        filters=["harmful", "restricted"],
        thresholds=0.7
    )
)

# Define workflow tools
@runtime.tool()
def check_approval_status(order_id: str) -> dict:
    """Check if order requires human approval"""
    return {"approved": True, "reviewer": "alice@company.com"}

implementation.description: Chain API calls with conditional logic for order processing workflow

implementation.description: Pause execution and notify human for approval decisions

implementation.title: Rollback Handler
implementation.description: Implement safe rollback for failed workflow steps

vexIntegration.title: Verify Action Correctness
vexIntegration.description: Ensure agent actions match workflow intent and business rules
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import verify, Guardrail

workflow_guardrail = Guardrail(
    name="workflow_action_correctness",
    checks=[
        "action.intent_matches_request",
        "response.schema_valid",
        "no_unsafe_operations"
    ]
)

async def execute_agent_turn(user_request: str):
    response = await runtime.user_turn(user_request)
    
    # Vex verification before committing
    verified = await verify(
        response,
        guardrails=[workflow_guardrail],
        context={"workflow_state": current_workflow_state}
    )
    
    if verified:
        return response
    else:
        await rollback_workflow()
        return "Action blocked by safety guardrails"

vexIntegration.title: Permission Validation Wrapper
vexIntegration.description: Validate user permissions before executing sensitive API calls

vexIntegration.title: Idempotency & Rollback Safety
vexIntegration.description: Ensure operations are idempotent with rollback capability tracking
vexIntegration.code:
idempotency_guardrail = Guardrail(
    name="idempotency_rollback",
    checks=[
        "operation.has_idempotency_key",
        "rollback_capability_tracked",
        "no_duplicate_execution"
    ]
)

async def safe_tool_execution(tool_call):
    # Pre-verify idempotency
    pre_check = verify(
        tool_call,
        guardrails=[idempotency_guardrail],
        context={"executed_keys": workflow_executed_keys}
    )
    
    if not pre_check.approved:
        return {"error": "Idempotency check failed"}
    
    # Track rollback capability
    result = await runtime.execute_tool(tool_call)
    track_rollback_capability(tool_call.id, result)
    
    return result
